name: Morse Law
description_short: Denver-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings with flat-fee pricing ($900 for Chapter 7) and full attorney representation throughout the process.
description_long:
Morse Law, LLC is a Denver-based bankruptcy law firm founded by Managing Attorney C. Todd Morse, located at 910 Sixteenth Street, Suite 1160 in Denver, Colorado. The firm specializes exclusively in personal bankruptcy representation and has completed over 8,000 Colorado bankruptcies. The firm positions itself as a high-volume, cost-conscious bankruptcy service provider rather than a budget document-preparation service.

Morse Law offers two primary bankruptcy filing options: Chapter 7 bankruptcy at a flat fee of $900 with full attorney representation, and Chapter 13 bankruptcy designed to stop foreclosure and help clients cure mortgage arrears. The firm also offers a $0 down filing option for employed individuals who can pay attorney fees post-filing at approximately $100/month or less. According to their website, the firm handles all aspects of bankruptcy representation, including initial consultation, petition drafting, document preparation, attendance at the 341 Meeting of Creditors, and case monitoring through completion.

Morse Law distinguishes itself primarily through aggressive price positioning. They advertise their $900 Chapter 7 fee as "about half" the fee charged by many competing firms and emphasize having "no exceptions and no catches." The firm explicitly states it provides "Full Attorney Representation" rather than limited scope or document-preparation services, meaning a licensed attorney handles the entire process. The $0 down option targets financially vulnerable clients facing immediate creditor action (wage garnishment, foreclosure) who cannot afford upfront fees. The firm also mentions potentially reducing car payments as part of the bankruptcy process.

While the website demonstrates clear pricing transparency and high case volume, potential clients should note that bankruptcy is complex and outcomes depend heavily on individual financial circumstances. The flat-fee model may incentivize faster case processing. The firm's emphasis on volume (8,000+ cases) and aggressive pricing suggests a high-throughput operation rather than boutique service. Clients should verify that full attorney representation includes adequate one-on-one time and personalized strategy development beyond standard case processing.
pros: Flat $900 fee for Chapter 7 bankruptcy with explicit "no exceptions and no catches" pricing guarantee
True full attorney representation (attorney drafts documents, attends 341 meeting, monitors case completion) rather than document-prep only
$0 down filing option available for employed individuals with monthly payments under $100
Over 8,000 completed Colorado bankruptcy cases demonstrates significant experience and volume
Free phone and office consultations available
Chapter 13 specialization includes stopping foreclosure immediately to preserve home ownership
Willingness to work on payment plans for clients unable to pay upfront fees
cons: High-volume operation (8,000+ cases) may limit personalized attention and strategy development per client
No information provided about attorney qualifications, credentials, or specific experience areas beyond total case count
Website contains a typographical error ("chpater 13") suggesting limited quality control on public-facing materials
Flat-fee model may create incentive to process cases quickly rather than spend time optimizing individual outcomes
No pricing information disclosed for Chapter 13 bankruptcies, only Chapter 7 ($900) and payment plan terms
best_for: Employed individuals facing immediate wage garnishment or foreclosure who need rapid bankruptcy filing but lack upfront attorney funds
Colorado residents seeking Chapter 7 bankruptcy with transparent, predictable costs and full attorney representation at below-market rates
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need Chapter 13 protection to stop the sale process and negotiate mortgage modifications
Bankruptcy filers who prioritize cost savings and prefer a high-efficiency firm over a small boutique practice
diagnosis: Morse Law is best suited for Colorado residents seeking Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy with straightforward circumstances and limited budgets who prioritize transparent pricing and rapid processing over boutique service. The primary caveat is that high-volume operations may limit individualized attention, and the flat-fee model may not accommodate complex financial situations requiring extensive strategic negotiation.
services: Creditor meeting preparation and attorney attendance at 341 Meeting of Creditors
Full case monitoring through case completion
Mortgage arrears negotiation and modification support
$0 down filing option with post-filing payment plans
Financial situation analysis and bankruptcy eligibility determination
Potential car payment reduction as part of bankruptcy process
Full attorney representation throughout bankruptcy process
